1607.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that a woman vowed to perform Hajj but then died. Her brother came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and asked him about it. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'If she had a debt, would you pay it?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Then fulfil the debt to Allah, for Allah is most deserving of fulfilment.'
1607.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang wanita bernazar untuk melaksanakan haji kemudian meninggal dunia. Saudaranya datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan menanyakannya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepadanya: 'Seandainya ia memiliki hutang, apakah kamu akan melunasinya?' Ia menjawab: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Maka tunaikanlah hutang kepada Allah, karena Allah paling berhak untuk dipenuhi.'

1608.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I said: O Messenger of Allah, I made a vow during the time of ignorance (jahiliyyah), and then Islam came.' He said: 'Fulfil your vow.'
1608.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, aku pernah bernazar di masa jahiliyah, kemudian Islam datang.' Beliau bersabda: 'Tunaikanlah nazarmu.'

1609.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) that his sister vowed to perform Hajj for Allah on foot without wearing a headscarf. He mentioned that to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he said: 'Tell your sister to wear her headscarf, and to ride, and to fast three days.'
1609.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa saudarinya bernazar untuk berhaji ke Rumah Allah dengan berjalan kaki tanpa berjilbab. Ia menyebutkan hal itu kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, dan beliau bersabda: 'Perintahkan saudarimu untuk berjilbab, menunggang (kendaraan), dan berpuasa tiga hari.'

1610.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the sister of Uqbah vowed to walk to the House (Kabah). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allah is indeed in no need of your sister's vow. Let her ride and offer a sacrifice.'
1610.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa saudari Uqbah bernazar untuk berjalan kaki menuju Rumah Allah (Kabah). Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Allah tidak membutuhkan nazar saudarimu. Biarlah ia menunggang (kendaraan) dan menyembelih hadyu.'

1611.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came across an old man being supported between his two sons. He said: 'What is the matter with this old man?' His two sons said: 'He vowed to walk.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Ride, for Allah is in no need of you and of your vow.'
1611.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melewati seorang laki-laki tua yang ditopang di antara dua anaknya. Beliau bersabda: 'Ada apa dengan orang tua ini?' Kedua anaknya berkata: 'Ia bernazar untuk berjalan kaki.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Naikilah kendaraan, karena Allah tidak membutuhkanmu dan nazarmu.'

1612.iwh_en: It was narrated from Imran ibn Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is no fulfilment of a vow in disobedience to Allah, nor in something that a person does not own.'
1612.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Imran ibn Husayn (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada kewajiban memenuhi nazar yang berisi maksiat kepada Allah, dan tidak pula nazar yang menyangkut sesuatu yang tidak dimiliki seseorang.'

1613.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever vows to obey Allah, let him obey Allah. And whoever vows to disobey Allah, let him not disobey Him.'
1613.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang bernazar untuk taat kepada Allah, maka hendaklah ia taat kepada Allah. Dan barangsiapa yang bernazar untuk bermaksiat kepada Allah, maka janganlah ia bermaksiat kepada-Nya.'

1614.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I vowed that if Allah granted you victory I would pray in Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem).' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Pray here.' He repeated it three times, and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'As you wish, then.'
1614.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku bernazar bahwa jika Allah memberikan kemenangan kepadamu, aku akan shalat di Bayt al-Maqdis (Yerusalem).' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalatlah di sini.' Ia mengulangi permintaannya tiga kali, dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Terserahlah kalau begitu.'

1615.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A vow does not ward off anything; it only extracts from the miser.'
1615.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Nazar tidak menolak sesuatu pun; ia hanya mengeluarkan (harta) dari orang yang kikir.'

1616.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came upon Umar ibn al-Khattab while he was riding in a caravan, swearing by his father. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Verily Allah forbids you to swear by your fathers. Whoever must take an oath, let him swear by Allah, or remain silent.'
1616.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melewati Umar ibn al-Khattab yang sedang berkendara dalam sebuah rombongan sambil bersumpah dengan nama ayahnya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Allah melarang kalian bersumpah dengan nama ayah-ayah kalian. Barangsiapa yang harus bersumpah, maka hendaklah ia bersumpah dengan nama Allah, atau diam.'

1617.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever takes an oath and then says: "If Allah wills," has made an exception (to the oath).'
1617.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang bersumpah dan kemudian berkata: "Insya Allah," maka ia telah membuat pengecualian (dari sumpah tersebut).'

1618.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever takes an oath and then says: "If Allah wills," he has the choice: if he wishes he may do it, and if he wishes he need not.'
1618.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang bersumpah dan kemudian berkata: "Insya Allah," maka ia memiliki pilihan: jika ia mau ia boleh melakukannya, dan jika ia mau ia tidak perlu melakukannya.'

1619.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said to Abu Bakr: 'Do not take an oath (on this matter).' — The hadith contains more detail.
1619.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata kepada Abu Bakr: 'Janganlah bersumpah (atas hal ini).' — Hadits ini memiliki perincian lebih lanjut.

1620.iwh_en: It was narrated from Adi ibn Hatim (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked him for something and he swore he would not give him anything. He then said: 'If I had not heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Whoever takes an oath and then sees something better than it, let him do what is better and make expiation for his oath," I would not have given him anything.'
1620.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Adi ibn Hatim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki memintanya sesuatu dan ia bersumpah tidak akan memberikannya apa pun. Kemudian ia berkata: 'Seandainya aku tidak mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang bersumpah kemudian melihat sesuatu yang lebih baik darinya, maka hendaklah ia melakukan yang lebih baik dan membayar kafarat sumpahnya," niscaya aku tidak akan memberikannya apa pun.'

1621.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abd al-Rahman ibn Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Abd al-Rahman ibn Samurah, do not seek authority, for if you are given it after asking for it you will be left to rely on yourself; but if you are given it without asking for it you will be helped in it. And if you take an oath and then see something better than it, make expiation for your oath and do what is better.'
1621.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abd al-Rahman ibn Samurah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai Abd al-Rahman ibn Samurah, janganlah kamu meminta jabatan kekuasaan, karena jika kamu diberi setelah memintanya kamu akan diserahkan kepada dirimu sendiri; tetapi jika kamu diberi tanpa memintanya kamu akan dibantu dalam menjalankannya. Dan jika kamu bersumpah kemudian melihat sesuatu yang lebih baik darinya, bayarlah kafarat sumpahmu dan lakukan yang lebih baik.'

1622.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sharid (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'My mother owes the freeing of a slave, and I have a black Nubian slave girl. Will she suffice?' He said: 'Call her.' He then asked her: 'Do you testify that there is no god but Allah?' She said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Free her, for she is a believer.'
1622.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sharid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Ibuku berkewajiban membebaskan seorang budak, dan aku memiliki seorang budak perempuan berkulit hitam dari Nubia. Apakah ia mencukupi?' Beliau bersabda: 'Panggillah ia.' Kemudian beliau bertanya kepadanya: 'Apakah kamu bersaksi bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah?' Ia menjawab: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Bebaskanlah ia, karena ia adalah seorang mukminah.'

1623.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Your oath is judged by what your companion (the one requesting it) believes you to mean.'
1623.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sumpahmu dinilai berdasarkan apa yang sahabatmu (orang yang memintanya) percaya kamu maksudkan.'

1624.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The customary oath of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that he used to swear by was: "No, by the Turner of hearts."'
1624.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Sumpah yang biasa digunakan Rasulullah ﷺ adalah: "Tidak, demi Dzat yang membolak-balikkan hati."'
